name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **TTF CRASHED -6.8% MAY 6 TO ~€47.4/MWh ON MOU SCOOP — PRICING PAUSE AS PLAUSIBLY DURABLE.** TTF -28% est on month; +35% est 3M (vs pre-crisis €32-35 baseline). The MOU's 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision included Qatar Ras Laffan reopening expectation in market pricing — TTF moved alongside Brent's -7% but with structural-anchored pricing (TTF moved less in both directions historically). TTF remains BELOW €70+ cascade-intensity threshold (5-sessions-sustained trigger NOT activated at any point in the May 4-7 stress event). Structural offsets intact: (a) Hormuz still functionally closed (5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm) but kinetic-war probability compressed; (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. **TTF-Brent decoupling persists** — paper TTF continues to behave more like inflation event than crisis event. If MOU signs, TTF could continue to compress toward €40-42 area; if Iran rejects, TTF re-bids back toward €52-55.

recent_signals.headline: TTF CRASHED -6.8% MAY 6 TO ~€47.4/MWh ON MOU SCOOP — pricing pause as plausibly durable. -28% est 1M / +35% est 3M. MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision priced Qatar Ras Laffan reopening expectation. TTF still below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; structural offsets (Hormuz closed, inventory tight, Ras Laffan 3-5yr recovery) intact.
